cityCenter.name: Central Berlin (Mitte)
cityCenter.description: Heart of Berlin around the Brandenburg Gate and Alexanderplatz

airports.name: Berlin Brandenburg
airports.terminals: T1, T2 (T5, the former Schönefeld, is closed and largely unused)

routes.name: FEX Airport Express

price.note: ABC zone ticket (BER is in zone C)

routes.tips: Fastest direct rail link to central Berlin. Departs from the station beneath Terminal 1. Buy an ABC ticket and validate it before boarding.
pros: Fastest direct train to the center

routes.name: S-Bahn S9

price.note: ABC zone ticket (BER is in zone C)

routes.destination: Central Berlin via Treptower Park & Ostkreuz toward Spandau
routes.tips: Slower than the FEX but stops at far more central stations and runs through the night. Same ABC ticket as all other options.

routes.name: Regional trains (RE/RB)

price.note: ABC zone ticket (BER is in zone C)

routes.tips: Regional RE/RB trains (e.g. RE8, RB23/RB24/RB32) call at BER and reach Alexanderplatz and Hauptbahnhof quickly. Check the platform departure board for the next service.

routes.name: Taxi

price.note: Metered fare; expect €50-70 to central Berlin (no fixed flat rate)

routes.tips: Official taxi ranks are outside Terminal 1. Fares are metered, not flat. Uber and Bolt operate at similar prices and can be cheaper off-peak.
cons: Far more expensive than the train

generalTips: BER is Berlin's only commercial airport—Tegel (TXL) closed in 2020 and Schönefeld (SXF) is now BER's largely unused Terminal 5. Don't go looking for the old airports.
BER sits in fare zone C, so you need an ABC ticket (about €4.70) for any train into the city—a normal AB city ticket is not valid for the airport leg.
There are no ticket barriers, but validate your ticket at the platform stamping machines and keep it—inspectors issue €60 fines for riding without a valid, validated ticket.
The FEX, S9, and regional trains all leave from the same station directly beneath Terminal 1; follow the train (DB / S-Bahn) signs down from arrivals.
For heavy sightseeing, the Berlin WelcomeCard (including the ABC airport zone) bundles unlimited transport with attraction discounts and can pay for itself quickly.
Download the BVG or VBB app for live departures and mobile tickets covering all operators on one fare.
